_note: Vancouver HQ data — BC Place, downtown Vancouver. 5 group stage matches + R32 + R16.

venue.name: BC Place
venue.notes: Retractable roof downtown stadium. Clear bag policy in effect. Remember: this is Canada — bring your passport if you're crossing from the US, and prices are in CAD.
venue.parkingNote: Downtown parking is scarce and expensive ($30–$60 CAD). SkyTrain is the only sensible option on match days.

transit.recommended: SkyTrain — Stadium–Chinatown station (Expo Line) is directly beside BC Place; Yaletown–Roundhouse (Canada Line) is a 10-minute walk
lines: Expo Line — Stadium–Chinatown station at the stadium's doorstep
Canada Line — Yaletown–Roundhouse, 10 min walk; direct from YVR airport
tips: Get a Compass card or tap a credit card directly at the gates — no ticket lines.
Canada Line runs direct from YVR airport to downtown in 25 minutes — no car needed all trip.
Stadium–Chinatown station gets crushed post-match; walking 10 min to Yaletown–Roundhouse is often faster.
US visitors: cell plans may treat Vancouver as international roaming — download offline maps.
transit.parking: Limited lots near Plaza of Nations and Concord Pacific from $30 CAD, sell out early. Park-and-ride from a suburban SkyTrain station beats downtown parking.
transit.rideshare: Uber/Lyft operate in Vancouver with designated pickup on Expo Blvd. Post-match surge is real but milder than US host cities — SkyTrain still wins.

fanZones.name: FIFA Fan Festival — PNE / Hastings Park
fanZones.location: Pacific National Exhibition grounds, East Vancouver
fanZones.notes: Vancouver's official Fan Festival site. Giant screens, food trucks, live music on the historic fairgrounds — 15 min from downtown on the Hastings bus corridor.

fanZones.name: Granville Street Entertainment District
fanZones.notes: Vancouver's bar strip becomes an unofficial fan corridor on match days. Expect street closures for the biggest fixtures.

preGame.name: Yaletown
preGame.notes: The closest pre-match district — patios, breweries, and sports bars along Mainland and Hamilton St. Fills early on match days.

preGame.name: Gastown
preGame.notes: Historic district with the city's best pub density. International supporters' groups have claimed bars here for the tournament.

preGame.name: Main Street / Chinatown
preGame.notes: Craft-beer corridor with a local feel. Quieter build-up before walking over to the stadium — and the best food value downtown.

cityEnergyForecast.crowdNote: Australia vs Turkey — Saturday night opener in downtown Vancouver. Turkish support should travel loudly, and the late kickoff turns Gastown and Granville into all-night football zones.
Canada vs Qatar — one of Canada's home dates at BC Place. This is when the city really starts to feel like a World Cup host.
New Zealand vs Egypt — Sunday evening, lower wattage on paper, but plenty of neutrals and a cleaner downtown flow than the Canada dates.
Switzerland vs Canada — Canada's second BC Place date and likely the loudest pure home crowd of the group stage in Vancouver.
New Zealand vs Belgium — Friday night with Belgium's pedigree and knockout implications. Strong draw, but not the all-timer this slot looked like before the final assignments.

transitPain.label: Moderate
transitPain.note: Australia vs Turkey — Saturday 9pm kickoff pushes the crowd later, but downtown will be busy all evening.
tips: SkyTrain from any suburban station beats driving downtown
Arrive by 7:30pm if you want time in Yaletown before kickoff
Post-match crowds will spill into downtown bars — leaving immediately will not save much time

transitPain.label: Moderate
transitPain.note: Canada vs Qatar — Thursday 3pm kickoff ends right at commute peak, and the home crowd adds another layer of demand.
tips: Expo Line trains packed in both directions post-match — consider the Yaletown–Roundhouse walk
Pre-match is easy; it's the 5pm exit that stings
Stay for dinner in Chinatown and skip the crush entirely

transitPain.label: Low
transitPain.note: New Zealand vs Egypt — Sunday 6pm is manageable by Vancouver standards, with lighter pre-match congestion than the Canada dates.
tips: Best day to test your route if attending multiple matches
Early dinner in Yaletown or Chinatown works better than a long pre-match brunch
Even driving is survivable today — but why

transitPain.label: High
transitPain.note: Switzerland vs Canada — Tuesday noon kickoff still packs downtown because Canada are in town. Expect lunchtime trains and a fast post-match release.
tips: Go in early from work or take a long lunch — entry lines build by 11am
Stadium–Chinatown station will queue at entry pre-match; buffer 30 min
Post-match, walk to Main Street for food and let the trains clear

transitPain.label: High
transitPain.note: New Zealand vs Belgium — Friday night with real demand, but not the maximum-strain scenario Vancouver expected before the draw.
tips: Be downtown by mid-afternoon and make a full evening of it
Post-match SkyTrain queues will be 30+ min at Stadium–Chinatown — walk to Yaletown–Roundhouse or wait it out in a bar
If staying outside downtown, pre-book transport now
Granville district will run past 2am — the city has waited decades for this

shouldIGoScores.label: Strongly Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: Australia vs Turkey — late Saturday kickoff, big Turkish support, and downtown Vancouver under the lights. Not the original glamour opener, but still a very good ticket.

shouldIGoScores.label: Strongly Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: Canada vs Qatar — one of two Canada dates at BC Place. If you want to feel this city actually hosting the World Cup, this is one of the tickets.

shouldIGoScores.label: Consider
shouldIGoScores.summary: New Zealand vs Egypt — less star power, but Sunday evening logistics are easy and the ticket should be relatively attainable by Vancouver standards.

shouldIGoScores.label: Strongly Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: Switzerland vs Canada — likely the best Canada atmosphere Vancouver gets in the group stage. Home crowd, real stakes, and a noon kickoff that keeps the day easy.

shouldIGoScores.label: Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: New Zealand vs Belgium — Friday night, solid football quality, and a cleaner buy than the most overhyped Vancouver scenarios. Strong option if you want a lively downtown match without Canada-ticket pricing.
